scope.selection_note: SYNTHETIC FIXTURE for the cross-topic / multiple-trails case. An ad-hoc release-hardening pass spanning two topics; because the artifact substrate supports only task-owned handles, the user explicitly selected t1187 as the owning task at creation. Ids, timestamps, and digests are frozen synthetic values.

narrative.problem_statement: Before the next release, which correctness fixes across the shadow and gate topics must land, regardless of each topic's own internal sequencing?
narrative.recommendation_summary: Two tasks qualify: the destructive-verification bug (t1187, shadow topic) and the red-suite fix (t1183, gate topic). Both also appear in their own topics' trails — this trail exists to give the release cut a single checklist. Each task keeps its one canonical topic; this trail merely references them.
narrative.method_note: Membership was selected by scanning both topics' open bugs for release-blocking severity; each task's canonical topic was read from its anchor resolution, not reassigned.
caveats: This trail intentionally overlaps trail-shadow-review-loop (t1187) and trail-gate-framework-landing (t1183). The board shows wave badges for exactly one selected trail at a time; overlap is data, not a display conflict.

waves.title: Release blockers
waves.purpose: The minimal cross-topic set that must land before the release cut.
waves.why_now: Both are user-visible correctness defects; the release date, not topic sequencing, drives their urgency here.

entries.rationale: Same task that appears in the shadow topic's own trail; for the release it is simply a must-fix correctness bug. Its canonical topic remains the shadow root — this trail references, never re-anchors.
entries.expected_outcome: Failed verifications keep their evidence in the released version.

entries.rationale: The red baseline suite must be green before a release can be cut with any confidence; it belongs to the gate topic but blocks the release globally.
entries.expected_outcome: Green suite at the release tag.

relations.note: A green suite makes the t1187 fix verifiable in CI; no ordering required.

exclusions.reason: Feature work, not a correctness fix; explicitly out of the release-blocking set even though its topic contributes a member.

evidence.summary: Destructive-verification bug; release-blocking severity, low effort.
Baseline suite still red; blocks any confident release cut.
